meaning: n.船上的煤仓,碉堡,掩体,燃料舱
examples: He put his second shot in a bunker to the left of the green.
The sandal is actually quite uncomfortable unless you're hiding in a bunker and have bigger worries, is the official symbol of the struggle.
Bunker Adjustment Factor reflects the movement in the marketplace for bunkers.
But I think it is important for us not to get into a bunker mentality.
Here, some 65 metres below the sundrenched streets of Moscow, lies a disused communications bunker.
